expectations.text: for_loop_used: A for / end loop drives mass and/or spring-damper creation
expectations.evidence: Two for/end loops: one iterating i=1..8 to create point masses, one iterating j=1..7 to create spring-dampers.

expectations.text: eval_function_used: EVAL() used to dereference a variable inside a command string
expectations.evidence: EVAL() used extensively, e.g. (eval(".chain_masses.mass_" // RTOI(i))), (eval((i - 1) * .chain_masses.spacing)), (eval(.chain_masses.stiffness)), etc.

expectations.text: rtoi_function_used: RTOI() used to convert the loop counter to a string for name construction
expectations.evidence: RTOI(i) and RTOI(j) used throughout both loops for name construction, e.g. RTOI(i), RTOI(j), RTOI(j + 1).

expectations.text: string_concat_operator_used: // string concatenation operator used to build part/marker names
expectations.evidence: // used in all name-building expressions, e.g. ".chain_masses.mass_" // RTOI(i) // ".cm", "sd_" // RTOI(j) // "_" // RTOI(j + 1).

expectations.text: point_mass_type_used: Masses created with part create point_mass (not rigid_body)
expectations.evidence: Loop body uses 'part create point_mass name_and_position' for each mass.

expectations.text: spring_damper_created: Spring-dampers created with force create element_like translational_spring_damper with stiffness=100, damping=1.0, displacement_at_preload=50
expectations.evidence: force create element_like translational_spring_damper used with stiffness=(eval(.chain_masses.stiffness)) [=100.0], damping=(eval(.chain_masses.damping)) [=1.0], displacement_at_preload=(eval(.chain_masses.free_len)) [=50.0].

expectations.text: first_mass_fixed_to_ground: First mass (mass_1) is constrained to ground with a SPHERICAL joint (NOT a fixed joint)
expectations.evidence: constraint create joint spherical with joint_name=.chain_masses.sph_fix, i_marker=.chain_masses.mass_1.cm, j_marker=.chain_masses.ground.anchor_mkr.
Loop runs from start_value=1 to end_value=(eval(.chain_masses.n_mass)) where n_mass=8.0, creating exactly 8 point masses.

expectations.text: 50mm_spacing: Masses are spaced 50 mm apart along the X axis
expectations.evidence: spacing=50.0; location=(eval((i - 1) * .chain_masses.spacing)), 0.0, 0.0 places mass_1 at x=0, mass_2 at x=50, ..., mass_8 at x=350.

expectations.text: simulate_transient: Script uses simulation single_run transient command
expectations.evidence: simulation single_run transient with type=auto_select, end_time=2.0, number_of_steps=2000.

expectations.text: dot_path_names: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: All names use full dot-path: .chain_masses.ground.anchor_mkr, .chain_masses.mass_1.cm, .chain_masses.sph_fix, .chain_masses.sd_1_2, etc.

expectations.text: no_adams_id: No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
expectations.evidence: No occurrence of 'adams_id' found anywhere in chain.cmd.

expectations.text: geometry_present: At least one geometry shape added to each mass
expectations.evidence: Inside the mass-creation loop, an ellipsoid (geometry create shape ellipsoid) is created for every mass using its .cm marker as center_marker.

expectations.text: adams_view_no_errors: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: run_adams_cmd.py exited 0. Simulation reached 'End Simulation' and 'batch run is finished' at t=2.0s. Two grep matches for 'Error:' are Python sys.excepthook traceback from Adams startup pythonrc.py (TypeError:/SyntaxError: in ! comment lines), not Adams ERROR: simulation errors.
